Center: US National Nuclear Data Center, Brookhaven, USA
ref: Jour: Physical Review, Part C, Nuclear Physics, Vol.112, p.024602 (2025)
title: Extraction of the non-spin- and spin-transfer isovector responses via the 12C(10Be,10B+gamma)12B reaction

x4freetext: Extraction of the non-spin- and spin-transfer
isovector responses via the 12C(10Be,10B+gamma)12B
reaction

x4codes.hlp: Michigan State University, East Lansing, MI, United States of America
Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory, Berkeley, CA, United States of America
Horia Hulubei National Institute (IFIN-HH), Magurele, Romania
University of Notre Dame, Notre Dame, IN, United States of America
Central Michigan University, Mt.Pleasant, MI, United States of America
RIKEN (Inst. of Physical and Chemical Research), Wako, Japan

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Physical Review, Part C, Nuclear Physics, Vol.112, p.024602 (2025)

x4codes.hlp: Michigan State University, East Lansing, MI, United States of America

x4freetext: The Coupled Cyclotron Facility of the
National Superconducting Cyclotron Laboratory (NSCL).
The 10Be beam was produced by impinging a 150 p nA,
120A MeV 18O beam on a 1316 mg/cm2 thick Be target at
the entrance of the A1900 fragment separator.
The S800 spectrograph focal-
plane detector system consisting of two cathode
readout drift chambers (CRDCs) installed 1 m apart,
an ionization chamber, and a 5 mm thick plastic
scintillator. The Gamma-Ray Energy Tracking In-beam
Nuclear Array (GRETINA).
A 56.33 mg/cm2 thick natC target (98.88% 12C).
The reaction products, including 10B from
the (10Be,10B) reaction, were magnetically analyzed for
momentum and detected in the S800 spectrograph
focal-plane detector system. The rearmost plastic
scintillator provided the trigger for the
data-acquisition system as well as the timing
information. The time of flight (TOF) of the reaction
products was deduced from the scintillator timing
relative to the radio frequency (RF) signal of the
cyclotrons. To separate the 10B ions from the other
reaction products, the energy loss in the ionization
chamber (dE) was plotted against the TOF of the
ejectiles, and gated on the residues of interest. The
energy, outgoing angles in the dispersive and
nondispersive directions, and the position in the
nondispersive direction at the target location were
deduced from the positions and angles in the focal
plane in a ray-tracing procedure using an inverse map
calculated with the ion-optical code cosy infinity.
To detect the gamma-rays emitted in-flight from the
de-excitation of the 10B ions with a high
signal-to-noise ratio, the GRETINA array was used.
Statistical uncertainties.
Approved by R.G.T.Zegers on December 19, 2025.
BP

x4headers.headerHelp: Energy of incident projectile, laboratory system

x4freetext: The differential cross sections were calculated
across the excitation-energy range of 0<Ex<50 MeV and
the scattering-angle range of 0<thetac.m.<3 degrees
in a missing-mass calculation. The double
differential cross sections were binned into 2 MeV
intervals in excitation energy in the original work,
and they were converted from mb/sr/2 MeV to mb/sr/MeV
units in EXFOR by dividing the original values by a
factor of 2.
